text: The surgeon incises the peritoneum over the pelvic promontory, with pneumoperitoneum aiding in visualizing the air tracking behind the superior rectal artery. The left iliac vein is carefully avoided due to its proximity. The hypogastric nerves are gently separated from the back of the superior rectal artery, with traction and counter-traction assisting in this process. The camera provides a steady view of the surgical field.

The surgeon uses a monopolar scissor in a brush-like technique to perform medial dissection along the side of the inferior mesenteric artery. Robotic arm III provides traction under the superior rectal artery, while the assistant uses a U-hand forceps for counter traction. The hypogastric nerve is visible at the pelvic brim, and the dissection continues parallel to the inferior mesenteric artery, revealing the left common iliac artery.
